" --append-system-prompt "You are an AI issue solver. When you investigate issues, prefer root-cause analysis. When you communicate, prefer facts you have checked yourself or cite sources that provide evidence, such as quoted code or references to documents or web pages. When you are unsure or working from assumptions, test them yourself or ask clarifying questions.
General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands and the output becomes large, save the logs to files for easier review.
   - When running commands, avoid setting a timeout yourself. Let them run as long as needed. The default timeout of 2 minutes is usually enough, and once commands finish, review the logs in the file.
   - When running sudo commands, especially package installations like apt-get, yum, or npm install, run them in the background to avoid timeout issues and permission errors when the process needs to be killed. Use the run_in_background parameter or append & to the command.
   - When CI is failing or user reports failures, consider adding a detailed investigation protocol to your todo list with these steps:
      Step 1: List recent runs with timestamps using: gh run list --repo Payel-git-ol/Octra --branch issue-107-a16883408ed8 --limit 5 --json databaseId,conclusion,createdAt,headSha
      Step 2: Verify runs are after the latest commit by checking timestamps and SHA
      Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo Payel-git-ol/Octra --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file with the Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says \"failing\" but tools show \"passing\", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
   - When you create debug, test, or example scripts while fixing an issue, keep them in ./examples and/or ./experiments so you can reuse them later.
   - When you test assumptions, keep experiment scripts in ./experiments.
   - When an experiment demonstrates a real-world use case of the software, add it to ./examples.
   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer.

Initial research.
   - When you start, create a detailed plan for yourself and follow your todo list step by step. Add as many relevant points from these guidelines to the todo list as practical so you can track the work clearly.
   - When the user mentions CI failures or asks to investigate logs, consider adding these todos to track the investigation: (1) list recent CI runs with timestamps, (2) download logs from failed runs to the ci-logs/ directory, (3) analyze error messages and identify the root cause, (4) implement a fix, (5) verify that the fix resolves the specific errors found in the logs.
   - When you read the issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
   - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use the Read tool to view and analyze it. Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify that the file is a valid image rather than HTML by using a CLI tool such as the 'file' command. When corrupted or non-image files, such as GitHub \"Not Found\" pages saved as `.png`, are read, they can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and crash the AI solver process. When the file command shows \"HTML\", \"text\", or \"ASCII text\", the download failed, so do not call Read on that file. Instead: (1) when images are from GitHub issues or PRs, such as URLs containing \"github.com/user-attachments\", retry with: curl -L -H \"Authorization: token \$(gh auth token)\" -o <filename> \"<url>\" (2) when the retry still fails, skip the image and note that it was unavailable.
   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/Payel-git-ol/Octra/issues/107.
   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner Payel-git-ol [keywords].
   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.
   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When the issue is not defined clearly enough, write a comment with clarifying questions.
   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.
   - When you are fixing a bug, find the actual root cause first and run as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and the code does not have enough tracing or logs, add them and keep them in the code with the default state switched off.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has three different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/Payel-git-ol/Octra/pulls/108/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/Payel-git-ol/Octra/issues/108/comments --paginate
      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/Payel-git-ol/Octra/pulls/108/reviews --paginate
      Note: The command \"gh pr view --json comments\" only returns conversation comments and misses review comments.
   - When you need the latest comments on the issue, use gh api repos/Payel-git-ol/Octra/issues/107/comments --paginate.

Solution development and testing.
   - When issue is solvable, first create a test that reproduces the problem, then implement the fix.
   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.
   - When coding, commit each atomic step that is useful on its own to the pull request branch so interrupted work remains preserved in the pull request.
   - When you test:
      start from testing of small functions using separate scripts;
      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.
   - When you test integrations, use existing framework.
   - When you test solution draft, include automated checks in pr.
   - When you write or modify tests, consider setting reasonable timeouts at test, suite, and CI job levels so failures surface quickly instead of hanging.
   - When you see repeated test timeout patterns in CI, investigate the root cause rather than increasing timeouts.
   - When the issue is unclear, write a comment on the issue with questions.
   - When you encounter problems that you cannot solve yourself and need human help, write a comment on the pull request asking for help.
   - When you need human help, use gh pr comment 108 --body \"your message\" to comment on existing PR.

Reproducible testing.
   - When fixing a bug, create a test that reproduces the problem before implementing the fix. When you cannot reproduce the problem, you cannot verify the fix.
   - When encountering logic bugs, write an automated test that fails due to the bug, then implement the fix to make it pass.
   - When encountering UI bugs, capture a screenshot showing the problem state, then create a visual regression test or manual verification screenshot after the fix.
   - When creating tests, prefer minimum reproducible examples, meaning the simplest test case that demonstrates the issue.
   - When submitting a fix, include in the PR description: (1) how to reproduce the issue, (2) the automated test that verifies the fix, (3) before/after screenshots for UI issues.
   - When a bug fix does not have a reproducing test, treat the fix as incomplete because regressions can occur later without notice.

Preparing pull request.
   - When you code, follow contributing guidelines.
   - When you commit, write clear message.
   - When you need examples of style, use gh pr list --repo Payel-git-ol/Octra --state merged --search [keywords].
   - When you open pr, describe solution draft and include tests.
   - When there is a package with version and GitHub Actions workflows for automatic release, update the version (or other necessary release trigger) in your pull request to prepare for next release.
   - When you update existing pr 108, use gh pr edit to modify title and description.
   - When you are about to commit or push code, run local CI checks first if they are available in contributing guidelines (like ruff check, mypy, eslint, etc.) to catch errors before pushing.
   - When you finalize the pull request:
      follow style from merged prs for code, title, and description,
      check that no uncommitted changes corresponding to the original requirements are left behind,
      check that the default branch is merged into the pull request branch,
      check that all CI checks are passing if they exist before you finish,
      check for latest comments on the issue and pull request to ensure no recent feedback was missed,
      double-check that all changes in the pull request address the original requirements of the issue,
      check for newly introduced bugs in the pull request by carefully reading gh pr diff,
      check that no previously existing features were removed without an explicit request in the issue description, issue comments, or pull request comments.
   - When you finish implementation, use gh pr ready 108.

Workflow and collaboration.
   - When you check branch, verify with git branch --show-current.
   - When you push, push only to branch issue-107-a16883408ed8.
   - When you finish, create a pull request from branch issue-107-a16883408ed8. (Note: PR 108 already exists, update it instead)
   - When you organize workflow, use pull requests instead of direct merges to default branch (main or master).
   - When you manage commits, preserve commit history for later analysis.
   - When you contribute, keep repository history forward-moving with regular commits, pushes, and reverts if needed.
   - When you face conflict that you cannot resolve yourself, ask for help.
   - When you collaborate, respect branch protections by working only on issue-107-a16883408ed8.
   - When you mention a result, include the pull request URL or comment URL.
   - When you need to create pr, remember pr 108 already exists for this branch.

Self review.
   - When you check your solution draft, run all tests locally.
   - When you check your solution draft, verify git status shows a clean working tree with no uncommitted changes.
   - When you compare with repo style, use gh pr diff [number].
   - When you finalize, confirm code, tests, and description are consistent.

GitHub CLI command patterns.
   - When fetching lists from GitHub API, use the --paginate flag to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
   - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
   - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/\${owner}/\${repo}/pulls/\${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.

Playwright MCP usage (browser automation via mcp__playwright__* tools).
   - When you develop frontend web applications (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, Angular, etc.), use Playwright MCP tools to test the UI in a real browser.
   - When WebFetch tool fails to retrieve expected content (e.g., returns empty content, JavaScript-rendered pages, or login-protected pages), use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for web browsing.
   - When WebSearch tool fails or returns insufficient results, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for internet search.
   - When you need to interact with dynamic web pages that require JavaScript execution, use Playwright MCP tools.
   - When you need to visually verify how a web page looks or take screenshots, use browser_take_screenshot from Playwright MCP.
   - When you need to fill forms, click buttons, or perform user interactions on web pages, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form).
   - When you need to test responsive design or different viewport sizes, use browser_resize from Playwright MCP.
   - When you finish using the browser, close it with browser_close to free resources.
   - When reproducing UI bugs, use browser_take_screenshot to capture the problem state before implementing any fix.
   - When fixing UI bugs, take before/after screenshots to provide visual evidence of the fix for human verification.
   - When creating UI tests, save baseline screenshots to the repository for visual regression testing.
   - When verifying UI fixes, compare screenshots to ensure the fix does not introduce unintended visual changes.

Visual UI work and screenshots.
   - When you work on visual UI changes (frontend, CSS, HTML, design), include a render or screenshot of the final result in the pull request description.
   - When you need to show visual results, take a screenshot and save it to the repository (e.g., in a docs/screenshots/ or assets/ folder).
   - When you save screenshots to the repository, use permanent links in the pull request description markdown (e.g., https://github.com/konard/Payel-git-ol-Octra/blob/issue-107-a16883408ed8/docs/screenshots/result.png?raw=true).
   - When uploading images, commit them to the branch first, then reference them using the GitHub blob URL format with ?raw=true suffix (works for both public and private repositories).
   - When the visual result is important for review, mention it explicitly in the pull request description with the embedded image.
   - When fixing UI bugs, capture both the \"before\" (problem) and \"after\" (fixed) screenshots as evidence for human verification.
   - When reporting UI bugs, include a screenshot of the problem state to enable visual verification of the fix.
   - When the fix is visual, include side-by-side or sequential comparison of before/after states in the PR description.
   - When possible, create automated visual regression tests to prevent the UI bug from recurring." | jq -c .)

   - When you read the issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.

Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify that the file is a valid image rather than HTML by using a CLI tool such as the 'file' command. When corrupted or non-image files, such as GitHub "Not Found" pages saved as `.png`, are read, they can cause "Could not process image" errors and crash the AI solver process. When the file command shows "HTML", "text", or "ASCII text", the download failed, so do not call Read on that file. Instead: (1) when images are from GitHub issues or PRs, such as URLs containing "github.com/user-attachments", retry with: curl -L -H "Authorization: token $(gh auth token)" -o <filename> "<url>" (2) when the retry still fails, skip the image and note that it was unavailable.
   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/Payel-git-ol/Octra/issues/107.
   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner Payel-git-ol [keywords].
   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.
   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When the issue is not defined clearly enough, write a comment with clarifying questions.
   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.
   - When you are fixing a bug, find the actual root cause first and run as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and the code does not have enough tracing or logs, add them and keep them in the code with the default state switched off.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has three different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/Payel-git-ol/Octra/pulls/108/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/Payel-git-ol/Octra/issues/108/comments --paginate
      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/Payel-git-ol/Octra/pulls/108/reviews --paginate
      Note: The command "gh pr view --json comments" only returns conversation comments and misses review comments.
   - When you need the latest comments on the issue, use gh api repos/Payel-git-ol/Octra/issues/107/comments --paginate.

Solution development and testing.
   - When issue is solvable, first create a test that reproduces the problem, then implement the fix.
   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.
   - When coding, commit each atomic step that is useful on its own to the pull request branch so interrupted work remains preserved in the pull request.
   - When you test:
      start from testing of small functions using separate scripts;
      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.
   - When you test integrations, use existing framework.
   - When you test solution draft, include automated checks in pr.
   - When you write or modify tests, consider setting reasonable timeouts at test, suite, and CI job levels so failures surface quickly instead of hanging.
   - When you see repeated test timeout patterns in CI, investigate the root cause rather than increasing timeouts.
   - When the issue is unclear, write a comment on the issue with questions.
   - When you encounter problems that you cannot solve yourself and need human help, write a comment on the pull request asking for help.
   - When you need human help, use gh pr comment 108 --body "your message" to comment on existing PR.

Reproducible testing.
   - When fixing a bug, create a test that reproduces the problem before implementing the fix. When you cannot reproduce the problem, you cannot verify the fix.
   - When encountering logic bugs, write an automated test that fails due to the bug, then implement the fix to make it pass.
   - When encountering UI bugs, capture a screenshot showing the problem state, then create a visual regression test or manual verification screenshot after the fix.
   - When creating tests, prefer minimum reproducible examples, meaning the simplest test case that demonstrates the issue.
   - When submitting a fix, include in the PR description: (1) how to reproduce the issue, (2) the automated test that verifies the fix, (3) before/after screenshots for UI issues.
   - When a bug fix does not have a reproducing test, treat the fix as incomplete because regressions can occur later without notice.

Preparing pull request.
   - When you code, follow contributing guidelines.
   - When you commit, write clear message.
   - When you need examples of style, use gh pr list --repo Payel-git-ol/Octra --state merged --search [keywords].
   - When you open pr, describe solution draft and include tests.
   - When there is a package with version and GitHub Actions workflows for automatic release, update the version (or other necessary release trigger) in your pull request to prepare for next release.
   - When you update existing pr 108, use gh pr edit to modify title and description.
   - When you are about to commit or push code, run local CI checks first if they are available in contributing guidelines (like ruff check, mypy, eslint, etc.) to catch errors before pushing.
   - When you finalize the pull request:
      follow style from merged prs for code, title, and description,
      check that no uncommitted changes corresponding to the original requirements are left behind,
      check that the default branch is merged into the pull request branch,
      check that all CI checks are passing if they exist before you finish,
      check for latest comments on the issue and pull request to ensure no recent feedback was missed,
      double-check that all changes in the pull request address the original requirements of the issue,
      check for newly introduced bugs in the pull request by carefully reading gh pr diff,
      check that no previously existing features were removed without an explicit request in the issue description, issue comments, or pull request comments.
   - When you finish implementation, use gh pr ready 108.

Workflow and collaboration.
   - When you check branch, verify with git branch --show-current.
   - When you push, push only to branch issue-107-a16883408ed8.
   - When you finish, create a pull request from branch issue-107-a16883408ed8. (Note: PR 108 already exists, update it instead)
   - When you organize workflow, use pull requests instead of direct merges to default branch (main or master).
   - When you manage commits, preserve commit history for later analysis.
   - When you contribute, keep repository history forward-moving with regular commits, pushes, and reverts if needed.
   - When you face conflict that you cannot resolve yourself, ask for help.
   - When you collaborate, respect branch protections by working only on issue-107-a16883408ed8.
   - When you mention a result, include the pull request URL or comment URL.
   - When you need to create pr, remember pr 108 already exists for this branch.

Self review.
   - When you check your solution draft, run all tests locally.
   - When you check your solution draft, verify git status shows a clean working tree with no uncommitted changes.
   - When you compare with repo style, use gh pr diff [number].
   - When you finalize, confirm code, tests, and description are consistent.

GitHub CLI command patterns.
   - When fetching lists from GitHub API, use the --paginate flag to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
   - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
   - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body "text" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body "text" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/${owner}/${repo}/pulls/${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.

Playwright MCP usage (browser automation via mcp__playwright__* tools).
   - When you develop frontend web applications (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, Angular, etc.), use Playwright MCP tools to test the UI in a real browser.
   - When WebFetch tool fails to retrieve expected content (e.g., returns empty content, JavaScript-rendered pages, or login-protected pages), use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for web browsing.
   - When WebSearch tool fails or returns insufficient results, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for internet search.
   - When you need to interact with dynamic web pages that require JavaScript execution, use Playwright MCP tools.
   - When you need to visually verify how a web page looks or take screenshots, use browser_take_screenshot from Playwright MCP.
   - When you need to fill forms, click buttons, or perform user interactions on web pages, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form).
   - When you need to test responsive design or different viewport sizes, use browser_resize from Playwright MCP.
   - When you finish using the browser, close it with browser_close to free resources.
   - When reproducing UI bugs, use browser_take_screenshot to capture the problem state before implementing any fix.
   - When fixing UI bugs, take before/after screenshots to provide visual evidence of the fix for human verification.
   - When creating UI tests, save baseline screenshots to the repository for visual regression testing.
   - When verifying UI fixes, compare screenshots to ensure the fix does not introduce unintended visual changes.

Visual UI work and screenshots.
   - When you work on visual UI changes (frontend, CSS, HTML, design), include a render or screenshot of the final result in the pull request description.
   - When you need to show visual results, take a screenshot and save it to the repository (e.g., in a docs/screenshots/ or assets/ folder).
   - When you save screenshots to the repository, use permanent links in the pull request description markdown (e.g., https://github.com/konard/Payel-git-ol-Octra/blob/issue-107-a16883408ed8/docs/screenshots/result.png?raw=true).
   - When uploading images, commit them to the branch first, then reference them using the GitHub blob URL format with ?raw=true suffix (works for both public and private repositories).
   - When the visual result is important for review, mention it explicitly in the pull request description with the embedded image.
   - When fixing UI bugs, capture both the "before" (problem) and "after" (fixed) screenshots as evidence for human verification.
   - When reporting UI bugs, include a screenshot of the problem state to enable visual verification of the fix.
   - When the fix is visual, include side-by-side or sequential comparison of before/after states in the PR description.
   - When possible, create automated visual regression tests to prevent the UI bug from recurring.

Every project is snapshotted into the [Nix](https://nixos.org) store, enabling instant rollback and zero-storage recovery.\n\n## Architecture\n\n> Full API specification for custom clients → [`docs/spec/`](docs/spec/OCTRA-SPECIFICATION.md)\n\n![Octra logic](docs/icons/octra-nix.png)\n\n```\nUser → API Gateway → Execution engine\n                        ├── Canvas workflow (user-defined) → direct pipeline\n                        │     ├── Universal node → single AI call\n                        │     └── Manager × N → Worker × N\n                        │\n                        └── Auto mode (no canvas)\n                              ├── AI complexity check (1-10)\n                              │     ├── trivial (≤2) → Universal node\n                              │     └── otherwise → Boss → Manager → Worker\n                              └── Agents Service → Claude / Gemini / GPT / ...\n```\n\n**Canvas workflow** — when the user places nodes on the canvas, Octra executes\nexactly what was laid out. No AI planning, no complexity grading, no fallback.\nThe user takes full responsibility for the architecture.\n\n**Auto mode** — Octra grades complexity via AI, then chooses the fast path\n(universal node for trivial tasks) or the full Boss → Manager → Worker pipeline.\n\n**Boss** plans architecture, spawns managers, validates output, pushes to GitHub.  \n**Managers** review and orchestrate workers.  \n**Workers** generate code inside Nix-isolated environments — either via AI or real tool scaffolding. \n\n## Universal node (trivial-task fast path)\n\nDivide-and-conquer is the right tool for complex projects, but the same machinery\nover-engineers trivial requests: *\"write hello world in Python\"* would spawn\nmanagers and workers and emit a tree of files with unclear logic instead of the one\nobvious line of code.\n\nOctra avoids this by routing trivial tasks through a single **universal node** —\none unconstrained AI call that returns the smallest correct result. The universal\nnode is available through two paths:\n\n### Explicit canvas node\n\nPlace a universal node on the canvas — Octra executes it directly, no questions\nasked. If the AI call fails, the task fails (no fallback to Boss). The user owns\nthe workflow.",
        "is_error": false
      }

Every project is snapshotted into the [Nix](https://nixos.org) store, enabling instant rollback and zero-storage recovery.\n\n## Architecture\n\n> Full API specification for custom clients → [`docs/spec/`](docs/spec/OCTRA-SPECIFICATION.md)\n\n![Octra logic](docs/icons/octra-nix.png)\n\n```\nUser → API Gateway → Execution engine\n                        ├── Canvas workflow (user-defined) → direct pipeline\n                        │     ├── Universal node → single AI call\n                        │     └── Manager × N → Worker × N\n                        │\n                        └── Auto mode (no canvas)\n                              ├── AI complexity check (1-10)\n                              │     ├── trivial (≤2) → Universal node\n                              │     └── otherwise → Boss → Manager → Worker\n                              └── Agents Service → Claude / Gemini / GPT / ...\n```\n\n**Canvas workflow** — when the user places nodes on the canvas, Octra executes\nexactly what was laid out. No AI planning, no complexity grading, no fallback.\nThe user takes full responsibility for the architecture.\n\n**Auto mode** — Octra grades complexity via AI, then chooses the fast path\n(universal node for trivial tasks) or the full Boss → Manager → Worker pipeline.\n\n**Boss** plans architecture, spawns managers, validates output, pushes to GitHub.  \n**Managers** review and orchestrate workers.  \n**Workers** generate code inside Nix-isolated environments — either via AI or real tool scaffolding. \n\n## Universal node (trivial-task fast path)\n\nDivide-and-conquer is the right tool for complex projects, but the same machinery\nover-engineers trivial requests: *\"write hello world in Python\"* would spawn\nmanagers and workers and emit a tree of files with unclear logic instead of the one\nobvious line of code.\n\nOctra avoids this by routing trivial tasks through a single **universal node** —\none unconstrained AI call that returns the smallest correct result. The universal\nnode is available through two paths:\n\n### Explicit canvas node\n\nPlace a universal node on the canvas — Octra executes it directly, no questions\nasked. If the AI call fails, the task fails (no fallback to Boss). The user owns\nthe workflow.",
    "stderr": "",
    "interrupted": false,
    "isImage": false,
    "noOutputExpected": false
  }

import.meta.env.VITE_API_GATEWAY_URL || '/api';\n\nexport interface UserRegisterRequest {\n  username: string;\n  email: string;\n  password: string;\n  captcha_token: string;\n}\n\nexport interface function getCurrentUser(accessToken: string): Promise<UserResponse> {\n  const response = await fetch(`${AUTH_API_URL}/me`, {\n    method: 'GET',\n    headers: {\n      'Authorization': `Bearer new Date(createdDate.getTime() + TRIAL_DAYS * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);\n  return now < trialEnd;\n}\n\nconst ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY = 'access_token';\nconst REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY = 'refresh_token';\n\n// Получаем removeAccessTokenCookie(): void {\n  document.cookie = `${ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY}=; path=/; SameSite=Lax; Max-Age=0`;\n}\n\nfunction storeTokens(accessToken: string, refreshToken: string): void {\n  localStorage.setItem(ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY, accessToken);\n  localStorage.setItem(REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY, refreshToken);\n  setAccessTokenCookie(accessToken);\n}\n\nfunction storeAccessToken(token: string): localStorage.removeItem(REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY);\n  removeAccessTokenCookie();\n}\n\ninterface AuthState {\n  user: User | null;\n  accessToken: string | null;\n  refreshToken: string | null;\n refreshToken: response.data.refresh_token,\n        isAuthenticated: true,\n        hasSubscription: false,\n        isInTrial: true, // New users start in trial\n        isLoading: false,\n        error: null,\n      });\n      // Check subscription status (new users don't have one)\n      try {\n        const userResponse = await getCurrentUser(response.data.access_token);\n        set({\n }\n    } catch (error) {\n      set({\n        isLoading: false,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Registration failed',\n      });\n      throw error;\n    }\n  },\n\n  login: isAuthenticated: true,\n          error: null,\n        });\n      } catch {\n        // ignore - will check on next page load\n      }\n    } catch (error) {\n      set({\n        isLoading: false,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Login failed',\n      });\n      throw error;\n    }\n  },\n\n  logout: async () => {\n    try {\n      await logoutUser();\n    } catch (error) {\n      console.error('Logout error:', error);\n    } finally {\n      clearUserScopedData();\n      removeTokens();\n      set({\n        user: null,\n        accessToken: null,\n        refreshToken: null,\n        isAuthenticated: false,\n        hasSubscription: false,\n        subscriptionEnd: null,\n        error: null,\n      });\n    }\n  },\n\n  setSubscription: (has: boolean) => {\n    set({ hasSubscription: has });\n  },\n\n  checkAuth: async () => {\n    const { accessToken, refreshToken } = get();\n\n    // Если нет access токена, пробуем обновить через refresh токен\n    if (!accessToken) {\n      if (refreshToken) {\n        try {\n          const response = await refreshAccessToken(refreshToken);\n          storeTokens(response.data.access_token,  if (!loginPassword) {\n      setFormError(t('auth.passwordRequired'));\n      return;\n    }\n\n    try {\n      await login(loginEmail, loginPassword);\n      onAuthSuccess();\n    } catch (err) {\n   setFormError(t('auth.usernameRequired'));\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (!regEmail.trim()) {\n      setFormError(t('auth.emailRequired'));\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const emailRegex = /^[^\\s@]+@[^\\s@]+\\.[^\\s@]+$/;\n    if (!emailRegex.test(regEmail)) {\n      setFormError(t('auth.invalidEmail'));\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (!regPassword) {\n      setFormError(t('auth.passwordRequired'));\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (regPassword.length < 6) {\n      setFormError(t('auth.passwordMinLength'));\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (regPassword !== base64.RawURLEncoding.DecodeString(payloadB64)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"bad payload encoding\")\n\t}\n\n\tvar claims LeFineClaims\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal(payloadBytes, &claims); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"bad payload json\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check audience\n\tif claims.Aud != \"octra\" {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"wrong audience\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check expiry\n\tif time.Now().Unix() > claims.Exp {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"code expired\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn &claims, nil\n}\n\nfunc splitTwo(s string, sep byte) []string {\n\tfor i := 0; i < len(s); i++ {\n\t\tif s[i] == sep {\n\t\t\treturn []string{s[:i], s[i+1:]}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn []string{s}\n}\n\n// RegisterLeFineRoutes registers the custom \"Login with LeFine\" flow (OAuth-like but simplified for internal use)\nfunc RegisterLeFineRoutes(r *gin.Engine) {\n\tr.GET(\"/auth/lefine\", func(c *gin.Context) {\n\t\tkefineBase := os.Getenv(\"LEFINE_BASE_URL\")\n\t\tif kefineBase == \"\" {\n\t\t\tkefineBase = \"https://lefine.pro\" // production default (use docker-compose override for local dev)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tredirectURI := os.Getenv(\"LEFINE_REDIRECT_URL\")\n\t\tif redirectURI == \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Default to current host + path (works for most deploys)\n\t\t\tscheme := \"https\"\n\t\t\tif c.Request.TLS == nil && os.Getenv(\"ENV\") != \"prod\" {\n\t\t\t\tscheme = \"http\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tredirectURI = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s://%s/auth/lefine/callback\", scheme, c.Request.Host)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tstate := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", time.Now().UnixNano()) // simple stateless state for now\n\n\t\tauthURL := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/oauth/authorize?client_id=octra&redirect_uri=%s&state=%s&response_type=code\",\n\t\t\tkefineBase,\n\t\t\turl.QueryEscape(redirectURI),\n\t\t\tstate,\n\t\t)\n\n\t\tc.Redirect(http.StatusTemporaryRedirect, authURL)\n\t})\n\n\tr.GET(\"/auth/lefine/callback\", func(c *gin.Context) {\n\t\tcode := c.Query(\"code\")\n\t\t// state := c.Query(\"state\") // we can verify state if we persist it later\n\n\t\tif code == \"\" {\n\t\t\tc.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{\"status\": \"error\", \"error\": \"No authorization code from LeFine\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tclaims, err := verifyLeFineCode(code)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tc.JSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{\"status\": \"error\", \"error\": \"Failed to verify LeFine code: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Create or get local Octra user from LeFine identity\n\t\tname := claims.DisplayName\n\t\tif name == \"\" {\n\t\t\tname = claims.Username\n\t\t}\n\t\tif name == \"\" {\n\t\t\tname = claims.Email\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tuser, err := services.GetOrCreateUserFromLeFine(claims.Email, name, claims.UserID)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tc.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{\"status\": \"error\", \"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\taccessToken, refreshToken, err := services.GenerateTokens(*user)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tc.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{\"status\": \"error\", \"error\": \"Failed to generate tokens\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tc.SetSameSite(2)\n\t\tc.SetCookie(\"refresh_token\", refreshToken, 604800, \"/\", \"\", false, true)\n\n\t\tfrontendURL := os.Getenv(\"FRONTEND_URL\")\n\t\tif frontendURL == \"\" {\n\t\t\tfrontendURL = \"https://octra.env.pm\"\n\t\t}\n\t\tc.Redirect(http.StatusTemporaryRedirect, frontendAppRedirectURL(frontendURL, accessToken, refreshToken))\n\t})\n}\n",

environment variable is not set\")\n\t}\n\n\ttoken, err := jwt.Parse(tokenString, func(token *jwt.Token) (interface{}, error) {\n\t\tif _, ok := token.Method.(*jwt.SigningMethodHMAC); !ok           "-  var user models.UserRegister",
          "-",
          "-  result := database.Db.Where(\"email = ?\", email).First(&user)",
          "-  if result.Error == nil {",
          "-    return &user, nil",
          "-  }",
          "-",
          "-  baseUsername := name",
          "-  if baseUsername == \"\" {",
          "-    if len(email) > 10 {",
          "-      baseUsername = email[:len(email)-10]",
          "-    } else {",
          "-      baseUsername = \"user_\" + uuid.New().String()[:8]",
          "-    }",
          "-  }",
          "-",
          "-  username := baseUsername",
          "-  var count int64",
          "-  database.Db.Model(&models.UserRegister{}).Where(\"username = ?\", username).Count(&count)",
          "-  if count > 0 {",
          "-    username = baseUsername + \"_\" + uuid.New().String()[:6]",
          "-  }",
          "-",
          "-  user = models.UserRegister{",
          "-    ID:       uuid.New(),",
          "-    Username: username,",
          "-    Email:    email,",
          "-  }",
          "-",
          "-  if err := database.Db.Create(&user).Error; err != nil {",
          "-    if strings.Contains(err.Error(), \"duplicate\") || strings.Contains(err.Error(), \"unique\") {",
          "-      user.Username = baseUsername + \"_\" + uuid.New().String()[:6]",
          "-      if err := database.Db.Create(&user).Error; err != nil {",
          "-        return nil, errors.New(\"Ошибка при создании аккаунта. Попробуйте ещё раз\")",
          "-      }",
          "-    } else {",
          "-      return nil, errors.New(\"Ошибка при создании аккаунта. Попробуйте ещё раз\")",
          "-    }",
          "-  }",
          "-",
          "-  return &user, nil",
          "+  return getOrCreateOAuthUser(email, name)",
          " }",

OAuth-only accounts have no password set — reject password login explicitly\n\tif user.Password == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"invalid email or password\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check password\n\terr It is the\n// shared implementation behind every OAuth/identity provider.\nfunc getOrCreateOAuthUser(email, name string) (*models.UserRegister, error) {\n\temail = NormalizeEmail(email)\n\n\tvar user models.UserRegister\n\tif err := database.Db.Where(\"email = ?\", email).First(&user).Error; err == nil {\n\t\treturn &user, nil\n\t}\n\n\tbaseUsername := strings.TrimSpace(name)\n\tif baseUsername == \"\" {\n\t\tif len(email) > 10 {\n\t\t\tbaseUsername = email[:len(email)-10]\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tbaseUsername = \"user_\" + uuid.New().String()[:8]\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tusername := baseUsername\n\tvar count int64\n\tdatabase.Db.Model(&models.UserRegister{}).Where(\"username = ?\", username).Count(&count)\n\tif count > 0 {\n\t\tusername = baseUsername + \"_\" + uuid.New().String()[:6]\n\t}\n\n\tuser = models.UserRegister{\n\t\tID:       uuid.New(),\n\t\tUsername: username,\n\t\tEmail:    email,\n\t}\n\n\tif err := database.Db.Create(&user).Error; err != nil {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(err.Error(), \"duplicate\") || strings.Contains(err.Error(), \"unique\") {\n\t\t\t// Race or username collision — retry once with a random suffix.\n\t\t\tuser.Username = baseUsername + \"_\" + uuid.New().String()[:6]\n\t\t\tif err := database.Db.Create(&user).Error; err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"Ошибка при создании аккаунта. {\n\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"unexpected signing method\")\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn []byte(secret), nil\n\t})\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tif !token.Valid {\n\t\treturn nil, {\n\trefreshSecret := os.Getenv(\"JWT_REFRESH_SECRET\")\n\tif refreshSecret == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"JWT_REFRESH_SECRET environment variable is not set\")\n\t}\n\n\ttoken, err := jwt.Parse(tokenString, func(token *jwt.Token) (interface{}, error) {\n\t\tif _, ok := token.Method.(*jwt.SigningMethodHMAC); !ok {\n\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"unexpected signing method\")\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn []byte(refreshSecret), nil\n\t})\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tif !token.Valid {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"invalid token\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn token, nil\n}\n\n// LoginUser authenticates user and returns tokens\nfunc LoginUser(req requests.UserLoginRequest) (map[string]interface{}, error) {\n\t// Find user by email\n\tvar user models.UserRegister\n\terr := database.Db.Where(\"email = ?\", NormalizeEmail(req.Email)).First(&user).Error\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"invalid email or password\")\n\t}\n\n\t// OAuth-only accounts have no password set — reject password login explicitly\n\tif user.Password == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"invalid email or password\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check password\n\terr = CheckPasswordHash(req.Password, user.Password)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"invalid email or password\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate tokens\n\taccessToken, refreshToken, err := GenerateTokens(user)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"access_token\":  accessToken,\n\t\t\"refresh_token\": refreshToken,\n\t\t\"user\":          userPublicInfo(user),\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// userPublicInfo returns the subset of user fields safe to expose to clients.\nfunc userPublicInfo(user models.UserRegister) map[string]interface{} {\n\treturn map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"id\":         user.ID.String(),\n\t\t\"username\":   user.Username,\n\t\t\"email\":      user.Email,\n\t\t\"created_at\": user.CreatedAt.Format(time.RFC3339),\n\t}\n}\n\n// getOrCreateOAuthUser finds an existing user by email or creates a new one,\n// generating a unique username when the desired one is already taken. It is the\n// shared implementation behind every OAuth/identity provider.\nfunc getOrCreateOAuthUser(email, name string) (*models.UserRegister, error) {\n\temail = NormalizeEmail(email)\n\n\tvar user models.UserRegister\n\tif err := database.Db.Where(\"email = ?\", email).First(&user).Error; err == nil {\n\t\treturn &user, nil\n\t}\n\n\tbaseUsername := strings.TrimSpace(name)\n\tif baseUsername == \"\" {\n\t\tif len(email) > 10 {\n\t\t\tbaseUsername = email[:len(email)-10]\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tbaseUsername = \"user_\" + uuid.New().String()[:8]\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tusername := baseUsername\n\tvar count int64\n\tdatabase.Db.Model(&models.UserRegister{}).Where(\"username = ?\", username).Count(&count)\n\tif count > 0 {\n\t\tusername = baseUsername + \"_\" + uuid.New().String()[:6]\n\t}\n\n\tuser = models.UserRegister{\n\t\tID:       uuid.New(),\n\t\tUsername: username,\n\t\tEmail:    email,\n\t}\n\n\tif err := database.Db.Create(&user).Error; err != nil {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(err.Error(), \"duplicate\") || strings.Contains(err.Error(), \"unique\") {\n\t\t\t// Race or username collision — retry once with a random suffix.\n\t\t\tuser.Username = baseUsername + \"_\" + uuid.New().String()[:6]\n\t\t\tif err := database.Db.Create(&user).Error; err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"Ошибка при создании аккаунта. Попробуйте ещё раз\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"Ошибка при создании аккаунта. Попробуйте ещё раз\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn &user, nil\n}\n\n// GetOrCreateUserFromGoogle creates or finds a user from Google OAuth\nfunc GetOrCreateUserFromGoogle(email, name string) (*models.UserRegister, error) {\n\treturn getOrCreateOAuthUser(email, name)\n}\n\n// GetOrCreateUserFromGithub creates or finds a user from GitHub OAuth\nfunc GetOrCreateUserFromGithub(email, name string) (*models.UserRegister, error) {\n\treturn getOrCreateOAuthUser(email, name)\n}\n\n// GetOrCreateUserFromLeFine creates or finds a user from LeFine/Kefine OAuth-like integration\nfunc GetOrCreateUserFromLeFine(email, name, lefineUserID string) (*models.UserRegister, error) {\n\treturn getOrCreateOAuthUser(email, name)\n}\n\n// RefreshTokens generates new tokens from refresh token\nfunc RefreshTokens(req requests.RefreshTokenRequest) (map[string]interface{}, error) {\n\t// Validate refresh token\n\ttoken, err := ValidateRefreshToken(req.RefreshToken)\n\tif err != nil token\")\n\t}\n\terr = database.Db.Where(\"id = ?\", userID).First(&user).Error\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"user not found\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate new tokens\n\taccessToken, refreshToken,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// GetMe returns current user info from access token\nfunc GetMe(tokenString string) (map[string]interface{}, error) {\n\ttoken, err := ValidateAccessToken(tokenString)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tclaims, ok := token.Claims.(jwt.MapClaims)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"invalid token time.Now().Add(RefreshTokenExpiry).Unix(),\n\t\t\"iat\":      time.Now().Unix(),\n\t\t\"type\":     \"refresh\",\n\t})\n\n\trefreshTokenString, err := environment variable is not set\")\n\t}\n\n\ttoken, err := jwt.Parse(tokenString, func(token *jwt.Token) (interface{}, error) {\n\t\tif _, ok := token.Method.(*jwt.SigningMethodHMAC); !ok {\n\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"unexpected signing method\")\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn []byte(secret), nil\n\t})\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tif !token.Valid {\n\t\treturn nil,           "+  body, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)",
          "+  var emails []struct {",
          "+    Email    string `json:\"email\"`",
          "+    Primary  bool   `json:\"primary\"`",
          "+    Verified bool   `json:\"verified\"`",
          "+  }",
          "+  if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &emails); err != nil {",
          "+    return \"\"",
          "+  }",
          "+",
          "+  // Prefer the primary verified email; otherwise any verified email.",
          "+  var firstVerified string",
          "+  for _, e := range emails {",
          "+    if e.Verified && firstVerified == \"\" {",
          "+      firstVerified = e.Email",
          "+    }",
          "+    if e.Primary && e.Verified {",
          "+      return e.Email",
          "+    }",
          "+  }",
          "+  return firstVerified",
          "+}",
          "+",
          " func RegisterGithubRoutes(r *gin.Engine) {",
          "   r.GET(\"/auth/github\", func(c *gin.Context) {",
          "     config := GetGithubOauthConfig()"
        ]
      }
    ],
    "userModified": false,
    "replaceAll": false
  }

TRIAL_DAYS = 14;\n\nfunction isUserInTrial(createdAt?: string): boolean {\n  if (!createdAt) return false;\n  const createdDate = new Date(createdAt);\n  const now = new Date();\n  const trialEnd = new Date(createdDate.getTime() + TRIAL_DAYS * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);\n  return now < trialEnd;\n}\n\nconst ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY = 'access_token';\nconst REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY = 'refresh_token';\n\n// Получаем токены из localStorage при инициализации\nfunction getStoredAccessToken(): string | null {\n  return localStorage.getItem(ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY);\n}\n\nfunction getStoredRefreshToken(): string | null {\n  return localStorage.getItem(REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY);\n}\n\nfunction setAccessTokenCookie(token: string): void {\n  document.cookie = `${ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY}=${token}; path=/; SameSite=Lax`;\n}\n\nfunction removeAccessTokenCookie(): void {\n  document.cookie = `${ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY}=; path=/; SameSite=Lax; Max-Age=0`;\n}\n\nfunction storeTokens(accessToken: string, refreshToken: string): void {\n  localStorage.setItem(ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY, accessToken);\n  localStorage.setItem(REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY, refreshToken);\n  setAccessTokenCookie(accessToken);\n}\n\nfunction storeAccessToken(token: string): void {\n  localStorage.setItem(ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY, token);\n  setAccessTokenCookie(token);\n}\n\nfunction removeTokens(): void {\n  localStorage.removeItem(ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY);\n  localStorage.removeItem(REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY);\n  removeAccessTokenCookie();\n}\n\ninterface AuthState {\n  user: User | null;\n  accessToken: string | null;\n  refreshToken: string | null;\n refreshToken: response.data.refresh_token,\n        isAuthenticated: true,\n        hasSubscription: false,\n        isInTrial: true, // New users start in trial\n        isLoading: false,\n        error: null,\n      });\n      // Check subscription status (new users don't have one)\n      try {\n        const userResponse = await getCurrentUser(response.data.access_token);\n        set({\n }\n    } catch (error) {\n      set({\n        isLoading: false,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Registration failed',\n      });\n      throw error;\n    }\n  },\n\n  login: response.data.refresh_token);\n          set({\n            accessToken: response.data.access_token,\n            refreshToken: response.data.refresh_token,\n            isAuthenticated: true,\n TRIAL_DAYS = 14;\n\nfunction isUserInTrial(createdAt?: string): boolean {\n  if (!createdAt) return false;\n  const createdDate = new Date(createdAt);\n  const now = new Date();\n  const trialEnd = new Date(createdDate.getTime() + TRIAL_DAYS * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);\n  return now < trialEnd;\n}\n\nconst ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY = 'access_token';\nconst REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY = 'refresh_token';\n\n// Получаем токены из localStorage при инициализации\nfunction getStoredAccessToken(): string | null {\n  return localStorage.getItem(ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY);\n}\n\nfunction getStoredRefreshToken(): string | null {\n  return localStorage.getItem(REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY);\n}\n\nfunction setAccessTokenCookie(token: string): void {\n  document.cookie = `${ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY}=${token}; path=/; SameSite=Lax`;\n}\n\nfunction removeAccessTokenCookie(): void {\n  document.cookie = `${ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY}=; path=/; SameSite=Lax; Max-Age=0`;\n}\n\nfunction storeTokens(accessToken: string, refreshToken: string): void {\n  localStorage.setItem(ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY, accessToken);\n  localStorage.setItem(REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY, refreshToken);\n  setAccessTokenCookie(accessToken);\n}\n\nfunction storeAccessToken(token: string): void {\n  localStorage.setItem(ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY, token);\n  setAccessTokenCookie(token);\n}\n\nfunction removeTokens(): void {\n  localStorage.removeItem(ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY);\n  localStorage.removeItem(REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY);\n  removeAccessTokenCookie();\n}\n\ninterface AuthState {\n  user: User | null;\n  accessToken: string | null;\n  refreshToken: string | null;\n set({ isLoading: true, error: null });\n    try {\n      const response = await registerUser({ username, email, password, captcha_token: captchaToken });\n      storeTokens(response.data.access_token, response.data.refresh_token);\n      set({\n        user: response.data.user,\n        accessToken: response.data.access_token,\n        refreshToken: response.data.refresh_token,\n        isAuthenticated: true,\n        hasSubscription: false,\n        isInTrial: true, // New users start in trial\n        isLoading: false,\n        error: null,\n      });\n      // Check subscription status (new users don't have one)\n      try {\n        const userResponse = await getCurrentUser(response.data.access_token);\n        set({\n          hasSubscription: userResponse.data.has_subscription || false,\n          subscriptionEnd: userResponse.data.subscription_end || null,\n          isInTrial: isUserInTrial(userResponse.data.created_at),\n          user: getCurrentUser(response.data.access_token);\n        set({\n          hasSubscription: userResponse.data.has_subscription || false,\n          subscriptionEnd: userResponse.data.subscription_end || refreshToken } = get();\n\n    // Если нет access токена, пробуем обновить через refresh токен\n    if (!accessToken) {\n      if (refreshToken) {\n        try {\n          const response = await getCurrentUser,\n  refreshToken as refreshAccessToken,\n  type User,\n} from '../services/authService';\nimport { clearUserScopedData } from './storageScope';\n\n// 14-day trial period\nconst TRIAL_DAYS = 14;\n\nfunction isUserInTrial(createdAt?: string): boolean {\n  if (!createdAt) return false;\n  const createdDate = new Date(createdAt);\n  const now = new Date();\n  const trialEnd = new Date(createdDate.getTime() + TRIAL_DAYS * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);\n  return now < trialEnd;\n}\n\nconst ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY = 'access_token';\nconst REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY = 'refresh_token';\n\n// Получаем токены из localStorage при инициализации\nfunction getStoredAccessToken(): string | null {\n  return localStorage.getItem(ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY);\n}\n\nfunction getStoredRefreshToken(): string | null {\n removeAccessTokenCookie(): void {\n  document.cookie = `${ACCESS_TOKEN_KEY}=; path=/; SameSite=Lax; Max-Age=0`;\n}\n\nfunction storeTokens(accessToken: string, refreshToken: string): void {\n localStorage.removeItem(REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY);\n  removeAccessTokenCookie();\n}\n\ninterface AuthState {\n  user: User | null;\n  accessToken: string | null;\n  refreshToken: string | null;\n  isAuthenticated: boolean;\n  hasSubscription: boolean;\n  subscriptionEnd: number | null;\n  isInTrial: boolean;\n  isLoading: boolean;\n  error: string | null;\n\n  // Actions\n  register: (username: string, email: string, password: string) => Promise<void>;\n  login: (email: string, password: string) => Promise<void>;\n  logout: () => Promise<void>;\n  checkAuth: () => Promise<void>;\n  setSubscription: (has: boolean) => void;\n  clearError: () => void;\n}\n\nconst initialToken = getStoredAccessToken();\nif (initialToken) {\n  setAccessTokenCookie(initialToken);\n}\n\nexport const error: null });\n    try {\n      const response = await registerUser({ username, email, password });\n      storeTokens(response.data.access_token, response.data.refresh_token);\n      set({\n        user: response.data.user,\n        accessToken: response.data.access_token,\n        refreshToken: response.data.refresh_token,\n        isAuthenticated: true,\n        hasSubscription: false,\n        isInTrial: true, // New users start in trial\n        isLoading: false,\n        error: null,\n      });\n      // Check subscription status (new users don't have one)\n      try {\n        const userResponse = await getCurrentUser(response.data.access_token);\n        set({\n          hasSubscription: userResponse.data.has_subscription || false,\n          subscriptionEnd: userResponse.data.subscription_end || null,\n          isInTrial: isUserInTrial(userResponse.data.created_at),\n          user: { ...response.data.user, subscription_end: userResponse.data.subscription_end },\n        });\n      } catch {\n        // ignore\n      }\n    } catch (error) {\n      set({\n        isLoading: false,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Registration failed',\n      });\n      throw error;\n    }\n  },\n\n  login: async (email: string, password: string) => {\n    set({ isLoading: true, error: null });\n    try {\n      const response = await loginUser({ email, password });\n      storeTokens(response.data.access_token, response.data.refresh_token);\n      set({\n        user: response.data.user,\n        accessToken: response.data.access_token,\n        refreshToken: response.data.refresh_token,\n        isAuthenticated: true,\n        hasSubscription: false,\n        isInTrial: false,\n        isLoading: false,\n        error: null,\n      });\n      // Immediately check subscription status from server\n      try {\n        const userResponse = await getCurrentUser(response.data.access_token);\n        set({\n          hasSubscription: userResponse.data.has_subscription || false,\n          subscriptionEnd: userResponse.data.subscription_end || null,\n          isInTrial: isUserInTrial(userResponse.data.created_at),\n          isAuthenticated: true,\n          error: null,\n        });\n      } catch {\n        // ignore - will check on next page load\n      }\n    } catch (error) {\n      set({\n        isLoading: false,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Login failed',\n      });\n      throw error;\n    }\n  },\n\n  logout: async () => {\n    try {\n      await logoutUser();\n    } catch (error) {\n      console.error('Logout error:', error);\n    } finally {\n      clearUserScopedData();\n      removeTokens();\n      set({\n        user: null,\n        accessToken: null,\n        refreshToken: null,\n        isAuthenticated: false,\n        hasSubscription: false,\n        subscriptionEnd: null,\n        error: null,\n      });\n    }\n  },\n\n  setSubscription: (has: boolean) => {\n    set({ hasSubscription: has });\n  },\n\n  checkAuth: async () => {\n    const { accessToken, refreshToken } = get();\n\n    // Если нет access токена, пробуем обновить через refresh токен\n    if (!accessToken) {\n      if (refreshToken) {\n        try {\n          const response = await   "type": "assistant",
  "message": {
    "model": "claude-opus-4-8",
    "id": "msg_017GoBwnzpjUn2S16W6o2R97",
    "type": "message",
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "tool_use",
        "id": "toolu_015cdUMQrxUjcYUsWz8YKTne",
        "name": "Bash",
        "input": {
          "command": "cat > /tmp/pr-body.md << 'EOF'\n## Summary\n\nFixes #107 — makes registration, authorization and OAuth work correctly and consistently across the API and the frontend.\n\nThe issue asked to \"fix the whole registration, authorization and OAuth so everything works perfectly, both via the API and on the frontend.\" Below are the concrete, root-caused bugs that were fixed.\n\n## Backend (`user` service)\n\n- **Email case mismatches caused login failures.** Registration stored the email verbatim while login matched it verbatim too, so `User@x.com` and `user@x.com` were treated as different accounts. Emails are now normalized (trim + lowercase) on **register**, **login** and **all OAuth flows**, so an account always resolves regardless of casing.\n- **Trial period never activated.** The frontend computes the 14‑day trial from `created_at`, but `/me`, `/login` and `/register` never returned it (so it was always `false`). All three now return `created_at` (RFC3339).\n- **OAuth-only accounts.** Password login against an account created via OAuth (empty password hash) is now rejected explicitly with the generic \"invalid email or password\".\n- **Server-side password policy.** The minimum length (6) is now enforced by the API, not only by the UI.\n- **OAuth CSRF.** The hardcoded `\"random-state\"` was replaced with a cryptographically random state stored in a short‑lived http‑only cookie and verified on every callback (Google, GitHub, LeFine).\n- **GitHub private email.** When GitHub hides the public email, the primary verified address is fetched from `/user/emails` before falling back to the synthetic `<login>@github.local`.\n- **Cleanup.** The three near-identical `GetOrCreateUserFrom{Google,Github,LeFine}` functions were consolidated into one helper; the unused `captcha_token` request fields were removed.\n\n## Frontend (`frontend/web`)\n\n- **Whole test suite was failing (pre-existing).** `authStore` touched `localStorage`/`document` at module-load time, which threw `ReferenceError: localStorage is not defined` in the Node/SSR evaluation used by the check scripts — crashing `npm test`. All such access in `authStore` and `storageScope` is now guarded for non-browser environments.\n- **Type/captcha cleanup.** Removed the leftover required `captcha_token` from the request types and the `register()` signature; added `created_at` to the `/me` response type.\n\n## How to reproduce / verify\n\n- Backend: `cd user && go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./...` — new tests cover token generation/validation, email normalization and OAuth state.\n- Frontend: `cd frontend/web && npm ci && npm test && npm run build` — `npm test` previously crashed; it now passes (exit 0), and the build succeeds.\n\n## Tests added\n\n- `user/internal/core/services/auth_test.go` — token round-trip, cross-secret rejection, missing-secret handling, refresh rejection, `NormalizeEmail`.\n- `user/internal/fetcher/http/oauth/state_test.go` — random state + `verifyState` matrix.\n- `user/internal/fetcher/http/router/accaunt/accaunt_test.go` — all auth routes are registered.\nEOF\ngh pr edit 108 --title \"Fix registration, authorization and OAuth (API + frontend)\" --body-file /tmp/pr-body.md && echo \"PR_UPDATED\"",

now.Unix(),\n\t\t\"type\":     \"access\",\n\t})\n\n\taccessTokenString, err := accessToken.SignedString([]byte(s.cfg.JWTSecret))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\trefreshToken := time.Time `json:\"created_at\"`\n}\n\nfunc (s *AuthService) generateTokens(user *model.User) (*LoginResult, error) {\n\tnow := time.Now()\n\n\taccessToken := jwt.NewWithClaims(jwt.SigningMethodHS256, now.Unix(),\n\t\t\"type\":     \"access\",\n\t})\n\n\taccessTokenString, err := accessToken.SignedString([]byte(s.cfg.JWTSecret))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\trefreshToken := jwt.NewWithClaims(jwt.SigningMethodHS256, jwt.MapClaims{\n\t\t\"user_id\":  user.ID.String(),\n\t\t\"username\": user.Username,\n\t\t\"email\":    user.Email,\n\t\t\"exp\": ValidateAccessToken(tokenString string) (jwt.MapClaims, error) {\n\ttoken, err := jwt.Parse(tokenString, func(token *jwt.Token) (interface{}, error) {\n\t\tif _, ok := token.Method.(*jwt.SigningMethodHMAC); !ok {\n\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"unexpected signing method\")\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn []byte(s.cfg.JWTSecret), nil\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, ValidateRefreshToken(tokenString string) (jwt.MapClaims, error) {\n\ttoken, err := jwt.Parse(tokenString, func(token *jwt.Token) (interface{}, error) {\n\t\tif _, ok := {
  "type": "user",
  "message": {
    "role": "user",
    "content": [
      {
        "tool_use_id": "toolu_01AKTfPM92ea7RBfbb3ysJxt",
        "type": "tool_result",
        "content": "Fix registration, authorization and OAuth (API + frontend)\n---BODY---\n## Summary\n\nFixes #107 — makes registration, authorization and OAuth work correctly and consistently across the API and the frontend.\n\nThe issue asked to \"fix the whole registration, authorization and OAuth so everything works perfectly, both via the API and on the frontend.\" Below are the concrete, root-caused bugs that were fixed.\n\n## Backend (`user` service)\n\n- **Email case mismatches caused login failures.** Registration stored the email verbatim while login matched it verbatim too, so `User@x.com` and `user@x.com` were treated as different accounts. Emails are now normalized (trim + lowercase) on **register**, **login** and **all OAuth flows**, so an account always resolves regardless of casing.\n- **Trial period never activated.** The frontend computes the 14‑day trial from `created_at`, but `/me`, `/login` and `/register` never returned it (so it was always `false`). All three now return `created_at` (RFC3339).\n- **OAuth-only accounts.** Password login against an account created via OAuth (empty password hash) is now rejected explicitly with the generic \"invalid email or password\".\n- **Server-side password policy.** The minimum length (6) is now enforced by the API, not only by the UI.\n- **OAuth CSRF.** The hardcoded `\"random-state\"` was replaced with a cryptographically random state stored in a short‑lived http‑only cookie and verified on every callback (Google, GitHub, LeFine).\n- **GitHub private email.** When GitHub hides the public email, the primary verified address is fetched from `/user/emails` before falling back to the synthetic `<login>@github.local`.\n- **Cleanup.** The three near-identical `GetOrCreateUserFrom{Google,Github,LeFine}` functions were consolidated into one helper; the unused `captcha_token` request fields were removed.\n\n## Frontend (`frontend/web`)\n\n- **Whole test suite was failing (pre-existing).** `authStore` touched `localStorage`/`document` at module-load time, which threw `ReferenceError: localStorage is not defined` in the Node/SSR evaluation used by the check scripts — crashing `npm test`. All such access in `authStore` and `storageScope` is now guarded for non-browser environments.\n- **Type/captcha cleanup.** Removed the leftover required `captcha_token` from the request types and the `register()` signature; added `created_at` to the `/me` response type.\n\n## How to reproduce / verify\n\n- Backend: `cd user && go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./...` — new tests cover token generation/validation, email normalization and OAuth state.\n- Frontend: `cd frontend/web && npm ci && npm test && npm run build` — `npm test` previously crashed; it now passes (exit 0), and the build succeeds.\n\n## Tests added\n\n- `user/internal/core/services/auth_test.go` — token round-trip, cross-secret rejection, missing-secret handling, refresh rejection, `NormalizeEmail`.\n- `user/internal/fetcher/http/oauth/state_test.go` — random state + `verifyState` matrix.\n- `user/internal/fetcher/http/router/accaunt/accaunt_test.go` — all auth routes are registered.",

🔍 Checking for pull requests from branch issue-107-a16883408ed8...
[{"createdAt":"2026-06-27T00:46:56Z","headRefName":"issue-107-a16883408ed8","isDraft":false,"number":108,"state":"OPEN","title":"Fix registration, authorization and OAuth (API + frontend)","updatedAt":"2026-06-27T01:09:46Z","url":"https://github.com/Payel-git-ol/Octra/pull/108"}]
  ✅ Found pull request #108: "Fix registration, authorization and OAuth (API + frontend)"
## Summary

Fixes #107 — makes registration, authorization and OAuth work correctly and consistently across the API and the frontend.

The issue asked to "fix the whole registration, authorization and OAuth so everything works perfectly, both via the API and on the frontend." Below are the concrete, root-caused bugs that were fixed.

## Backend (`user` service)

- **Email case mismatches caused login failures.** Registration stored the email verbatim while login matched it verbatim too, so `User@x.com` and `user@x.com` were treated as different accounts. Emails are now normalized (trim + lowercase) on **register**, **login** and **all OAuth flows**, so an account always resolves regardless of casing.
- **Trial period never activated.** The frontend computes the 14‑day trial from `created_at`, but `/me`, `/login` and `/register` never returned it (so it was always `false`). All three now return `created_at` (RFC3339).
- **OAuth-only accounts.** Password login against an account created via OAuth (empty password hash) is now rejected explicitly with the generic "invalid email or password".
- **Server-side password policy.** The minimum length (6) is now enforced by the API, not only by the UI.
- **OAuth CSRF.** The hardcoded `"random-state"` was replaced with a cryptographically random state stored in a short‑lived http‑only cookie and verified on every callback (Google, GitHub, LeFine).
- **GitHub private email.** When GitHub hides the public email, the primary verified address is fetched from `/user/emails` before falling back to the synthetic `<login>@github.local`.
- **Cleanup.** The three near-identical `GetOrCreateUserFrom{Google,Github,LeFine}` functions were consolidated into one helper; the unused `captcha_token` request fields were removed.

## Frontend (`frontend/web`)

- **Whole test suite was failing (pre-existing).** `authStore` touched `localStorage`/`document` at module-load time, which threw `ReferenceError: localStorage is not defined` in the Node/SSR evaluation used by the check scripts — crashing `npm test`. All such access in `authStore` and `storageScope` is now guarded for non-browser environments.
- **Type/captcha cleanup.** Removed the leftover required `captcha_token` from the request types and the `register()` signature; added `created_at` to the `/me` response type.

## How to reproduce / verify

- Backend: `cd user && go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./...` — new tests cover token generation/validation, email normalization and OAuth state.
- Frontend: `cd frontend/web && npm ci && npm test && npm run build` — `npm test` previously crashed; it now passes (exit 0), and the build succeeds.

## Tests added

- `user/internal/core/services/auth_test.go` — token round-trip, cross-secret rejection, missing-secret handling, refresh rejection, `NormalizeEmail`.
- `user/internal/fetcher/http/oauth/state_test.go` — random state + `verifyState` matrix.
- `user/internal/fetcher/http/router/accaunt/accaunt_test.go` — all auth routes are registered.
---

### A note on the base branch

This PR originally targeted `create/new-concept`, but that branch is a parallel full rewrite of the project (the `user/` Go service is restructured into `backend/` with a repository pattern, and the Vite/React frontend is replaced by a Next.js app under `frontend/web/app`). The auth fixes here are authored against the **current default branch `master`**, where the bugs actually exist and apply cleanly — so the base was retargeted to `master`. Most of the backend fixes in this PR (email normalization, username-uniqueness checks, OAuth-only login rejection) are already independently implemented in `create/new-concept`'s rewritten service, and its frontend is a different codebase, so these changes are not portable to it.
